name: Gravel tour to the Natural Park of Las Ubiñas-La Mesa along the Senda del Oso (Bear Trail)
name_original: Vuelta gravel al Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa por la Senda del Oso
region_intro: Gravel biking around Proaza offers routes through verdant landscapes shaped by the Trubia River and its fertile valley. The region features significant natural elements such as gorges, including Peñas Juntas, and disused mining railway lines like the Senda del Oso. These paths traverse a varied terrain of mountains, valleys, and forests, characteristic of Asturias's rugged beauty. Best gravel bike trails around Proaza The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel tour to the Natural Park of Las Ubiñas-La Mesa along the Senda del Oso (Bear Trail), a 45.0 miles (72.5 km) trail that takes 8 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows sections of the Senda del Oso, leading towards the Natural Park of Las Ubiñas-La Mesa. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Mine Railway Tunnel – Stone Bridge of Proaza loop from Proaza, a difficult 27.8 miles (44.8 km) path. This loop explores historical railway tunnels and passes the Stone Bridge of Proaza. Local gravel bikers also love the Stone Bridge of Proaza – Peñas Juntas Gorge loop from Trubia, a 21.9 miles (35.3 km) trail leading through the Peñas Juntas Gorge, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Gravel biking around Proaza is defined by green valleys, river paths along the Trubia, and routes through gorges and disused railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present challenging terrain. The routes in Proaza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 60 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Proaza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are there in Proaza?
A: Proaza offers a selection of over 4 gravel bike trails, primarily featuring challenging routes through its diverse landscapes. These trails are highly rated by the komoot community, indicating a rewarding experience for those exploring the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Proaza?
A: The most enjoyable seasons for gravel biking in Proaza are &lt;strong&gt;spring, summer, and autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. Each season presents unique natural beauty, with lush green landscapes in spring and summer, and vibrant colors in autumn. Winter conditions can be more challenging due to the rugged terrain of Asturias.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly gravel bike trails in Proaza?
A: While many routes in Proaza are rated as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;, the region's star attraction, the Senda del Oso (Bear's Trail), offers sections that are relatively gentle with slight elevation differences. This disused railway line is generally accessible, though some connected routes can be more challenging. It's advisable to check individual route details for specific difficulty levels.

Q: Can I find family-friendly gravel biking options in Proaza?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent option for families, as it follows a disused railway line, making it generally flat and suitable for various skill levels. Sections like the 6 km stretch from Tuñón to Proaza or the 8 km path from Proaza to Valdemurio are good choices. The trail is even equipped with gravel bike rental services, making it convenient for a family outing.

Q: Are there circular gravel bike routes available in Proaza?
A: Yes, Proaza offers several circular gravel bike routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40006959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mine Railway Tunnel – Stone Bridge of Proaza loop from Proaza&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 44.8 km route, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40637098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge of Proaza – Peñas Juntas Gorge loop from Trubia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.3 km long. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40636712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bear Trail – Vicenturio High Pasture loop from Samartín&lt;/a&gt;, a 29.7 km loop.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see while gravel biking in Proaza?
A: Proaza is located in the 'Valley of the Bear' and is deeply involved in the conservation of the endangered Cantabrian brown bear. While spotting wild bears is rare, you can visit the &lt;strong&gt;bear enclosure&lt;/strong&gt; about 1 km from Proaza, where rescued bears like Paca and Molina can sometimes be seen. The region's rich biodiversity also means you might encounter various bird species and other local fauna in its verdant landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks along the gravel routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes follow the Trubia River, offering views of its fertile valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40637098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge of Proaza – Peñas Juntas Gorge loop from Trubia&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the prominent &lt;strong&gt;Peñas Juntas Gorge&lt;/strong&gt;. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/347031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Alba&lt;/a&gt; or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/825310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Gamoniteiro&lt;/a&gt;, a significant peak in the area.

Q: How accessible are the gravel bike trails in Proaza by public transport?
A: While specific public transport details for trailheads can vary, Proaza is a municipality in Asturias, a region with some public transport infrastructure. For the Senda del Oso, which runs through the heart of Proaza, it's advisable to check local bus schedules to nearby towns like Tuñón or Trubia, which serve as common starting points. Some gravel bike rental services are also available in the area.

Q: Where can I find parking near the gravel bike trails in Proaza?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Buyera recreational area&lt;/strong&gt;, located near Proaza, is a convenient spot for cyclists as it offers dedicated parking facilities. This area also provides sports facilities and dining options, making it a practical starting or ending point for your gravel biking adventure.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about their experience in Proaza?
A: The gravel bike trails in Proaza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.8 stars from over 4 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Reviewers often praise the region's green valleys, river paths along the Trubia, and the adventurous routes through gorges and disused railway lines, highlighting the challenging yet rewarding terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that offer cafe stops or places to rest?
A: The Senda del Oso, being a popular route, has several points of interest along its path. The &lt;strong&gt;Buyera recreational area&lt;/strong&gt; near Proaza is a known stop offering dining options. Additionally, Proaza itself is a town with amenities, and other villages along the Senda del Oso, such as Tuñón or Entrago, may offer local cafes or restaurants for a refreshing break.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for gravel bike trails in Proaza?
A: Gravel bike trails in Proaza vary in length and duration, catering to different levels of endurance. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1668089357/gravel-tour-to-the-natural-park-of-las-ubinas-la-mesa-along-the-senda-del-oso-bear-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel tour to the Natural Park of Las Ubiñas-La Mesa along the Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 72.5 km route, typically taking over 8 hours. Shorter options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40006959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mine Railway Tunnel – Stone Bridge of Proaza loop from Proaza&lt;/a&gt; at 44.8 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40637098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge of Proaza – Peñas Juntas Gorge loop from Trubia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.3 km and can be completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced gravel bikers in Proaza?
A: Yes, Proaza is well-suited for experienced gravel bikers seeking a challenge. All listed routes are rated as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring significant elevation gains and varied terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1668089357/gravel-tour-to-the-natural-park-of-las-ubinas-la-mesa-along-the-senda-del-oso-bear-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel tour to the Natural Park of Las Ubiñas-La Mesa along the Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, for example, involves over 1800 meters of elevation gain over 72.5 km, offering a demanding ride through Asturias's rugged beauty.
tips:
Following the route of the old mining train, we arrive at this spectacular passage along the Senda del Oso.
Moderate your speed: remember that we share the road with pedestrians.
Picturesque reservoir in the valley of the Río Quirós.

Puerto de Ventana is a mountain pass located in the Cantabrian Mountains, between the Principality of Asturias and the province of León. It lies at an altitude of 1,587 meters and connects the municipality of San Emiliano in León with the Asturian municipality of Teverga.

From the pass, you can enjoy a breathtaking view of the San Emiliano Valley, known as Babia de Yuso.
A really nice stretch of road. It is not usually very busy with motor vehicles, even so, be alert at every curve: with such beauty it is easy to get lost and change lanes.
